title: Express Middleware, Promise Errors, Dependencies, and Response Ownership · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for express middleware, promise errors, dependencies, and response ownership and demands the smallest safe composed middleware, dependency, and error boundary with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for composed middleware, dependency, and error boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compose request identity, trust, limits, parsing, authentication, authorization, application, error, and observation middleware in a defended order.
Use Express 5 automatic Promise rejection forwarding while preserving causal error identity and avoiding duplicate next calls.
Implement four-argument error middleware, normalized failure taxonomy, disclosure policy, and headersSent delegation without double writes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Middleware Order Short Circuit · changed case · Quiz Middleware Order Short…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 8014 in a administrative API crossing two identity and network trust zones. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to trace defended middleware order while keeping error and response ownership explicit; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then change the method and preserve both status and durable-effect evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
